prompt: Create a charming 1930s Fleischer Studios–inspired rubber-hose cartoon illustration of a smiling young woman wearing a cream hijab riding a small tan pony toward the viewer in a sunny park. The rider sits front-facing, slightly leaning forward with a sweet, playful expression, large vintage cartoon eyes, long lashes, rosy cheeks, red lips, and simplified rounded features; she wears a pale yellow collared blouse, black pants, and white sneakers. The pony has an oversized cute face, big oval eyes, a blond shaggy mane, floppy ears, a white blaze, brown bridle and reins, rounded hooves, and a friendly exaggerated expression. Place them centered in the foreground on a green grassy field with small tufts of grass and a soft shadow beneath the pony. In the background, include exactly 4 small human figures: two seated picnic guests on the left, one standing man waving near the center-left, and one standing woman in a beige dress on the right. Add exactly 3 visible lawn-game objects on the grass: a white cornhole board, a blue cone, and two colored sticks lying flat. Surround the scene with rounded dark green trees on both sides and in the distance, with exactly 4 puffy cream clouds in a muted blue sky. Use thick black ink outlines, cel-animation shapes, rubber-hose proportions, hand-painted watercolor texture, aged paper grain, slightly faded colors, subtle film speckles, and a nostalgic vintage cartoon mood. Keep the composition vertical, storybook-like, wholesome, and whimsical; no modern photorealism, no text, no watermark.
